task_completion.description: The underexposed image is opened in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing application (e.g., GIMP, Photoshop, default OS photo viewer/editor) is open, displaying a dark, underexposed portrait image.

task_completion.description: The brightness adjustment tool or panel is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, side panel, or menu specifically for adjusting 'Brightness' (or 'Exposure') is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The brightness value is increased using the tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The brightness slider is moved to the right, or a positive numerical value is entered in the brightness input field.

task_completion.description: The image visibility is noticeably improved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main image canvas shows the portrait is visibly brighter, revealing more details in the dark areas compared to the initial state.

task_completion.description: The image maintains tonal balance without excessive highlight clipping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The brightened image looks natural and does not have large patches of pure white (blown-out highlights). Facial details and textures remain visible and are not washed out.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical user interface to adjust the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with a GUI image editor's menus and sliders, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent edited the correct original image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image in the editor is the specific underexposed portrait provided for the task, not a substitute image or a pre-edited version downloaded from the web.

integrity.description: The brightness change is a result of genuine tool usage.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual change in the image corresponds to the active adjustment of the brightness/exposure sliders in the UI, rather than just opening a different, already-brightened file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is an underexposed portrait. A successful adjustment must visibly increase brightness (programmatic threshold is >= 10% increase) while preserving quality and tonal balance. Look for a natural-looking result that avoids excessive highlight clipping (programmatic threshold is < 5% pure white/clipped pixels). The adjustment should be meaningful but not extreme.
